# Kaola-Workflow Roadmap

This file mirrors active unfinished work. GitHub issues are the source of truth when available.

## Active Work

| Issue | Title | Status | Workflow Project | Next Step |

| #729 | bug(replan): empty or uncovered failed-review findings can activate a review-only child epoch | PARTIALLY SHIPPED — 4 of 9 acceptance criteria met (case (a) empty-frontier refusal 8370e577; AC2 finding frontier in the planner packet 7f2340c3); IN FLIGHT as WAVE A of epic #757 | — | SHIPPED (case a): reviewSourceCarriesFrontier + typed refusal replan_source_findings_missing, bound at every reachable frontier (source read, authority verification, planner packet, stored-transaction entry at both phases, candidate re-author); 8/8 mutants killed; recovery row added to the repair-routing card. SHIPPED (AC2): the source finding frontier is carried into the planner packet, indexed under packet.source.finding_index — the key was renamed from an earlier colliding form rather than editing an agent surface; verified across 48 mutants with 40 killed and 8 low/non-blocking survivors. TWO LOAD-BEARING SCOPING CALLS to preserve in any follow-up — the guard is scoped to authority_kind review_outcome because diagnosis_to_build legitimately carries findings [] (pinned by a killed mutant), and the predicate is non-empty FINDINGS not non-empty OPEN frontier, because a progress-only failure legitimately carries an all-resolved frontier whose record of what failed is intact. REMAINING (5 of 9 ACs): AC3 child carry-forward reporting uncovered uid/path/node; AC4 review-only child cannot activate for an unresolved path-based finding; AC5 correctly mapped writer-to-certifier still activates; AC6 N-1 of N coverage refuses; AC7 crash-prefix/idempotent retry at the new coverage seam. FAIL-DIRECTION WARNING for the remaining work: this invariant asks whether a source packet is SAFE to spend an epoch on, so it must answer NO unless the packet is PROVABLY covered — absence of a field is the corruption signature it exists to catch, never an implicit pass. Do NOT reuse unresolvedInScopeFixes (the fail-OPEN gate predicate); adaptive-schema.js now also carries repairResponsibleFindings (fail-CLOSED); read both comments before choosing, and write a third predicate if neither answers this question. Case (b) SEMANTIC COVERAGE deliberately unimplemented with the soundness argument recorded inline: every mechanical proxy at this seam is unsound (an anchor records where a defect was OBSERVED and may legally carry no path; fix_role is advisory not contractual; "child has any writer" does not even catch the live repro). A sound check needs the child plan to DECLARE which inherited finding uids each writer owns — a plan-grammar addition in the validator; AC2 was its prerequisite and has now landed. Minor: repairCandidateReauthorPrefix performs durable writes before the guard on one resume path (epoch is NOT consumed and phase does not advance, so AC1 holds, but "zero mutation" is not literally true there). SEQUENCING — co-opens with #759 in Wave A on named evidence: this work is confined to replan.js while #759 is confined to adaptive-node.js and next-action.js. EPIC NOTE (#757): the finding-frontier planner packet is the brief substrate for #761 — the shape is already consumable outside the replan seam, so #761 is not blocked on the remaining ACs. HARDENING BEFORE MERGE (Wave A, from the adversarial pass — verdict ACCEPT with 14/21 mutants killed, blocking=[]): two survivors are genuine coverage gaps rather than defence-in-depth and are closed IN THIS WAVE, because #761 rewrites replan.js in Wave B and the tests get much more expensive to add after the file moves. (1) M7 — duplicate finding_owners: meta lines are entirely untested, although refusing a duplicate is the stated reason metaFieldOccurrences exists; the existing check('F1=w,,F1=w') case pins an empty token inside ONE line, not a repeated line. (2) M18/M19 — the coverage wall is pinned only in AGGREGATE: the early pre-freeze check in resumeReplanUnlocked and the call in validateChildPlan each survive individual removal, so neither placement is independently regression-protected and the documented ordering property (docs/api.md: the verdict is taken on the attested image before any child byte is written) has no assertion. The remaining survivors (M13/M13b certifier reachability and every/some, M17 source-identity guard, M20 unresolvable-certifier) are accepted as defence-in-depth on the verifier's reasoning: each is unreachable through the canonical lane, and M13b was proven independently refused by the validator G1 rule before the coverage wall runs. NOTE the verifier CONTRADICTED the implementer here — the implementer claimed M6 certifier-reachability-removed was KILLED and it was not. |
| #730 | bug(repair-dispatch): reopened fixer receives IDs but not a digest-bound canonical finding brief | 9 of 10 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA SHIPPED 2026-07-22 (merge 2bf87e7e) — only AC-9 remains; SCHEDULED WAVE C of epic #757 | — | REMAINING — AC-9 only: direct repair and replan-child repair must use the SAME feedback-delivery contract. The digest-bound brief is seeded on the DIRECT repair-node path only; a writer reopened by a replan CHILD (kaola-workflow-replan.js) still receives the inherited frontier WITHOUT the brief and must read the reviewer evidence named in .cache/replan-source.json. The gap is recorded as an explicit scope limit in docs/plan-run-cards/repair-routing.md (commit c4c1ba66) — it is deferred, not overlooked. SEQUENCING — held to WAVE C on named S1 evidence: this rewrites the replan-child feedback path in replan.js, and #761 (Wave B) retires replan_source_journal_missing in that same file, so the artifact it consumes does not settle until #761 lands. THE THIRD REJECTION AND ITS ROOT CAUSE — worth carrying because it nearly shipped twice: the ownership partition was built on unresolvedInScopeFixes, which is the GATE predicate. It requires EXPLICIT scope==='in_scope' && action==='fix' and is deliberately fail-OPEN so that omission cannot manufacture a blocker. Repair asks the OPPOSITE question and must fail CLOSED. A schema-1 flat row such as 'finding: id=F-A status=open severity=high file=scripts/a.js' carries neither scope nor action, so the gate set excluded it and the brief silently truncated to a single-writer assignment — the exact defect the issue forbids. Fixed by adding repairResponsibleFindings to adaptive-schema.js: exclusion by POSITIVE PROOF of non-blocking only (explicitly resolved/deferred status, explicitly non-in_scope scope, or explicitly non-fix action); a row missing those fields is INCLUDED. Mutation-proven — reverting to the gate predicate reds 6 assertions, with the payload showing consumed_by='impl-b' and assigned_uids=['F-B'] while F-A, owned by impl-a, sits in context_findings. LESSON TO APPLY TO EVERY REMAINING CHILD: state at each predicate which question it answers and which direction it fails, and pin BOTH directions with a case that OMITS the optional fields entirely. EPIC NOTE (#757): the digest-bound canonical brief is the fixer brief for #761 — the constructor is already callable outside repair-dispatch; no scope change. |
| #745 | bug(plan-validator): interior code-reviewer/security-reviewer walls still false-block on disjoint downstream writes — the surface-freshness rescue covers only adversarial-verifier, and their reopen remedy still dead-ends | queued — epic #757 WAVE D; closes with a DISPOSITION NOTE, no implementation (reopen-remedy half superseded by #761) | — | SHIPPED: interiorSurfaceFresh widened to code-reviewer/security-reviewer, with the producer slice RECOMPUTED from the frozen graph (not trusted from the journal) and a final certifier counting as a live whole-candidate wall only when its ledger row is actually complete. REMAINING: the reopen remedy. Auto-parking a "provably pristine" sink was REJECTED as unsafe, not deferred — the sink runs main-session-direct and its first irreversible step writes no in-band marker, so a pristine sink cannot be distinguished from one that already committed/pushed/merged. A safe remedy needs an explicit sink-progress marker. Cross-epoch freshness (seal attempt absent after journal rotation) also still falls back to whole-candidate. RESHAPED 2026-07-22 under epic #757: the reopen-remedy half is SUPERSEDED by #761 (local re-expansion; reopen becomes rollback-only) — do not implement it here. When #762 lands, re-check whether the shipped freshness widening is still load-bearing: declared+amended-surface staleness removes the whole-candidate binding at the root, and the cross-epoch residual dissolves with it. |
| #756 | bug(finalize/reopen): finalize done-gate surfacing a required file outside a completed writer's frozen write set wedges a PASSED-review serial run (reopen strands, amend orphans journal, replan needs a failed attempt) | queued — epic #757 WAVE D; acceptance HARNESS for #761 + #762, not an independent fix | — | RESHAPED 2026-07-22 under epic #757 — this issue is now the ACCEPTANCE HARNESS for #761 (local re-expansion: removes dead-ends 3/4/6, no failed-attempt prerequisite) + #762 (declared-not-walled surfaces: removes dead-ends 1/2/5); do NOT batch with the #745 reopen-remedy half (superseded by #761). Of the three candidate directions, direction 1 was chosen and generalized; direction 2 (tail-first cascade) is unnecessary (reopen stays rollback-only); direction 3 (completeness lint) is downgraded to an optional planner aid. A run whose review PASSED wedges at finalize when a project done-gate (not modeled as a DAG node) surfaces a required code-relevant file outside every frozen write set: barrier-check refuses write_set_overflow, verdict-check refuses verdict_not_pass (the code-relevant file stales the certifier candidate; docs correctly do not), reopen-node on the writer refuses would_strand_completed_dependent, on the gate refuses would_orphan_in_progress then would_strand, write-set amend + freeze --repair refuses review_journal_plan_hash_mismatch, and replan refuses replan_source_journal_missing because it requires a FAILED-review attempt a passed review never produces. Three candidate directions in the issue: (1) a sanctioned expand-and-re-review primitive that does not require a prior failed attempt, (2) reopen-node tail-first cascade instead of a hard would_strand refusal, (3) a planner companion-file completeness lint. Acceptance requires the recovery keep the new file REVIEWED, not merely attributed. |
| #757 | epic(adaptive): progressive elaboration — coarse frozen spine + just-in-time expansion, parallel-by-default | IN PROGRESS — child #758 (spine grammar) SHIPPED and CLOSED 2026-07-22 (merge 0ecb1421, all four chains green with captured per-chain exit codes); remaining children scheduled as five waves | — | EXECUTION SCHEDULE — five waves, run STRICTLY IN ORDER. WAVE A (running): #759 expansion transaction (adaptive-node.js, next-action.js) ∥ #729 remaining ACs (replan.js). WAVE B: #761 local re-expansion (adaptive-node.js, replan.js) ∥ #760 serialization inversion (adaptive-schema.js, routing skeletons+slots, adaptive-node.js) ∥ #763 context packet + efficiency line (adaptive-node.js). WAVE C: #762 declared-not-walled surfaces (plan-validator.js, adaptive-schema.js, adaptive-node.js) ∥ #730 AC-9 unify direct-repair and replan-child feedback delivery (replan.js). WAVE D: #766 opencode/kimi port sweep (additive trees only) ∥ #756 acceptance harness for #761+#762 (walkthrough) ∥ #745 disposition close (docs only). WAVE E: #765 cutover ALONE, then close this epic. CRITICAL PATH is #759 → #761 → #762 → #766 → #765 and every edge on it is a NAMED S1 data dependency, so the wave count cannot be compressed below five: #761 consumes the expansion-record shape from #759; #762 routes amendments to re-review THROUGH #761's re-expansion record; #766 ports the settled prose of all core children; #765 ends dual-accept only after a full run has completed on the spine shape. Everything off that path is scheduled at the earliest wave its own dependency allows — #760 and #763 need only #759, so they co-open with #761 in WAVE B rather than waiting on it. CO-OPEN EVIDENCE — #761/#760/#763 all write adaptive-node.js, but shared-file-ness is a conflict FORECAST, not a serializer: S1 requires naming a concrete artifact one unit consumes from another, and none of the three consumes anything from the other two. They co-open in isolated legs and reconcile at the join; merge order is #761 (largest, region-replacing) then #763 (smallest, additive) then #760, so each reconcile is against a settled base. #730 AC-9 is held to WAVE C on real S1 evidence, not caution: it rewrites the replan-child feedback path in replan.js, and #761 retires replan_source_journal_missing in that same file — the artifact is named, so it waits. CARRY-FORWARD RULE (binding for every wave) — a wave is not finished when its code merges; it is finished when everything it DISCOVERED has a named owner. Each producer must return descoped items explicitly, and each descoped item must be written into the roadmap source of the child that will own it BEFORE the producing wave merges. Never leave a discovery only in a workflow journal or an agent transcript: those are ephemeral and this epic has already generated four such items. If a discovery fits no existing child, file a GitHub issue and add its .roadmap source in the same action. A verifier finding that is argued non-blocking still counts as a discovery and still needs an owner — the #758 spine validation-policy escape was carried into #759 this way and closed there. WAVE A CARRY-FORWARD, already assigned: #759's four descopes went to #761 (expansion_id as a validated review-journal attempt field), #762 (composed gate units invisible to --gate-verify / --verdict-check, fails closed), #763 (workflow-tasks.json mirror omits composed units, cosmetic), and #765 (six workflow-init surfaces + resume/frontier-batch cards move as one write set with the cutover); #729's surviving mutants are hardened in Wave A itself before merge. Per-child planning deltas live as 2026-07-22 comments on each issue. Principle prose was propagated ahead of mechanics at commit ad196273 (axioms+embeds, planner md+tomls, plan-run skeleton ×12) with chains DEFERRED — that debt is DISCHARGED as of the #758 ship, which ran all four chains green. Migration is dual-accept (legacy full-DAG plans keep byte-identical behavior); the cutover decision is an ADR at epic close. Hard anchors that must NOT loosen: consent valve, guard prologue, atomic freeze, reviewed-before-sink wall, finalize receipts, running-set ledger atomicity, unattributed-write tamper refusal. STRATEGY (user directive 2026-07-22): evidence-backed serialization — serial requires present-tense nameable evidence for S1/S2/S3; predictions and uncertainty are inadmissible; mode is evidence-governed, width stays with faithful decomposition (see the #760 addendum). |
| #759 | feat(adaptive-node): expansion transaction — open-time frontier composition recorded append-only in the ledger, resume-safe | ATTEMPTED AND REJECTED 2026-07-23 (adversarial pass, 23/30 mutants killed, 4 blocking findings) — branch wave-a-759 NOT merged; rework required before Wave B can start, because #761 consumes this shape | — | New adaptive-node subcommand expand-open: takes units[] {role, tier, write surfaces, mode serial\|co_open, dependency edges} + the derivation lines (grain/path/join/probe/serializer), appends an EXPANSION RECORD (append-only, monotonically numbered per point) to the ledger, then opens via the EXISTING running-set scheduler. Guard prologue applies. Crash between append and open reconciles via reconcile-running-set roll-forward (idempotent). next-action.js ready-set derivation reads spine + expansion records. Review journal rebinds to (spine plan_hash, expansion id) so re-expansion never orphans a completed journal. Re-expansion is the SAME transaction — no separate machinery. DELTA 2026-07-22: the six workflow-init surfaces are IN SCOPE (their generated CLAUDE.md describes freeze + Node Ledger + resume-reads-the-ledger) plus docs/workflow-state-contract.md and the resume/frontier-batch plan-run cards; model-tier resolution moves from next-action.js plan-lookup to units carrying tier at open time — name the single resolver owner. REWORK REQUIRED 2026-07-23 — four blocking findings, all reproduced end to end on a clean tree; the attempt is on branch wave-a-759 and is worth reworking rather than discarding (AC4 four chains genuinely green, AC5 spine validation-policy fail-close well built, the transaction skeleton is sound). B1 HARD WEDGE: a composed code-reviewer unit is a LEGAL composition (validateComposition reserves only finalize / main-session-gate / expansion-point) but OPENS through the freeze view with a schema-1 dispatch (no contract_version, no review_context_hash) and CLOSES through the execution view's schema-2 review path — close-node refuses review_context_mismatch permanently, expand-close refuses scheduler_active, reconcile-running-set no-ops; one live node, no ready work, no legal transition. The byte-identical SPINE reviewer in the same plan closes fine. Fix is compose-time (reserve the gate roles) or widen the review-context seam. B2 AC2 HALF-DONE: the Phase-2-to-3 crash sub-window (frontier opened, open() proof block missing) is NOT repaired — reconcile-running-set returns not_opening and never restores the proof, while expand-open/expand-close refuse expansion_open_incomplete carrying repair: 'run reconcile-running-set', an operator instruction that is a NO-OP in exactly the state it is emitted for. The reconciled-path arm sits BELOW the not_opening early return, so it is unreachable for a stable running set. B3 TEST INSUFFICIENCY on the load-bearing crash-repair logic: 5 of 7 survivors re-confirmed against the FULL walkthrough (231 scenarios) AND full test-adaptive-node (2932 assertions) — expand-close's ENTIRE guard prologue is unpinned (integrity, consent-halt fence, scheduler/serial exclusion all removable invisibly), expand-open's integrity + consent-halt fence are unpinned (only excl is pinned), the reconciled-path roll-forward arm is dead code in every test, and AC2's idempotence assertion passes via an unrelated not_opening early return rather than the guard it claims to pin. B4: AC1's close->sink tail is SIMULATED by literal ledger string replacement (simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js content.replace of pending->complete), so no test runs the real close lifecycle of a schema-2 review gate downstream of an expanded milestone — which is precisely why B1 was not caught. BLAST RADIUS is bounded: no shipped surface authors plan_form: spine or calls expand-open, so none of this is reachable from a real run today — that bounds urgency, not correctness. DESCOPE NOTE CORRECTION: the producer's claim that composed gate units 'fail CLOSED — simply invisible' is factually wrong (see B1); the corrected item moved back here from #762. |
| #760 | feat(protocol): serialization-trigger inversion — co-open by default at open time; serial requires a named serializer | queued — epic #757 WAVE B (co-opens with #761 + #763; owns adaptive-schema.js + routing skeletons/slots, touches adaptive-node.js) | — | Invert the burden of proof: mode defaults to CO-OPEN in isolated per-leg worktrees; serial only on a NAMED serializer backed by PRESENT-TENSE EVIDENCE (user directive 2026-07-22) — S1 true data dependency (name the concrete artifact B consumes from A, or co-open), S2 shared irreversible external effect (name the shared resource — resource identity, not a conflict forecast), S3 no worktree support (the failed probe result). Predictions/anticipations/uncertainty are INADMISSIBLE grounds for serial. Evidence lines are audit-only derivations — the only mechanical check permitted is that a line EXISTS; no validator parses content. Governs MODE only: width stays with faithful decomposition (grain merges under-sized units). UNCERTAIN overlap is NOT a serializer (co-open isolated + mandatory synthesizer reconcile; extends the existing containment+reconcile correctness net from proven-disjoint to uncertain). Grain adjusts unit COARSENESS, never mode. --write-overlap-consent narrows to the S2 class. Propagate to the SIX routing surfaces via skeletons+slots regeneration (never hand-edit the 12 generated surfaces); zero uncertain-implies-serial language may remain; route-reachability + all four contract validators green. DELTA 2026-07-22: taxonomy completion — proven same-file overlap = MERGE the units (grain response), serial-with-named-path as fallback; KAOLA_PARALLEL_WRITES=0 survives as operator serializer (classify with S3); PROTECTED files fold into S2/consent, post-dominating-gate condition discharged by the spine review wall; rewrite surfaces include slots.js frontmatter descriptions + token pins. Posture block already in the skeleton at ad196273 — this child replaces the mechanics prose around it. |
| #761 | feat(adaptive-node): local re-expansion — any finding re-expands its owning expansion point; no failed-attempt prerequisite | queued — epic #757 WAVE B, CRITICAL PATH (co-opens with #760 + #763; owns adaptive-node.js re-expansion + replan.js retirements; merge FIRST of the three) | — | A finding from ANY source — failed review, PASSED-review candidate staleness, finalize done-gate (#756 trigger), out-of-surface attribution (#762) — routes to local re-expansion of the owning expansion point: a new expansion record with at minimum {fixer unit, re-review unit}; reviewed-before-sink preserved by construction. Fixer brief = digest-bound canonical brief (#730 shape) built from the finding frontier (#729 shape). Ownership by declared+amended surface; spanning findings re-expand each owner; a finding outside every milestone's scope is the ONLY remaining epoch-replan trigger. Retire as primary remedies: unique-maximal-producer escalation to replan for interior findings, the reopen strand/orphan family (reopen = rollback only), the replan_source_journal_missing failed-attempt prerequisite. AC: the #756 repro reaches a passing REVIEWED finalize with no hand-edits; the #745 reopen dead-end resolves. DELTA 2026-07-22: finalize re-expansion is admissible only BEFORE the sink's first irreversible step (commit/push/merge), determined from DERIVED sink-progress evidence (sink writes no in-band marker); after that it is a follow-up claim. The #729 empty-frontier refusals bind unchanged at spine-level replans. CARRY-FORWARD FROM #759 (Wave A descope, owner named by the producer): make expansion_id a VALIDATED field inside review-journal attempts. #759 shipped the pairing (expand-open emits review_binding {plan_hash, expansion_id}; each record carries the spine plan_hash it was composed against; the walkthrough pins that hash invariant across a re-expansion) but did NOT add the field to the attempt schema, because that schema lives in adaptive-schema.js — the byte-identical-x4 drift anchor — and gates every existing schema-1 and schema-2 review, so it would have to be optional-and-ignored to avoid invalidating in-flight journals. THIS child is the first consumer that actually needs to ask which record an attempt reviewed, so it lands here. Do not re-litigate the #759 scoping; implement the field. |
| #762 | feat(barrier): declared-not-walled write surfaces — append-only surface amendments with attribution; overflow becomes attribution + re-review | queued — epic #757 WAVE C (co-opens with #730 AC-9; owns plan-validator.js barrier + certifier staleness) | — | Write surfaces become declarations for attribution/review-scoping, not walls. Out-of-surface companion file → append a surface AMENDMENT to the owning expansion record + route to re-review via #761; write_set_overflow stops refusing attributable source-tree files. Certifier candidate digest/staleness computed against declared+AMENDED surfaces (invalidates exactly the re-review unit, no whole-candidate staling). HARD anchors kept: unattributed out-of-surface write at sink = fail-closed tamper refusal; consent-gated curated/irreversible roots (S2) still require the consent valve — amendment loosens attribution, never consent. AC: #756 dead-ends 1/2/5 gone in the repro; tamper case still refuses. DELTA 2026-07-22: the KAOLA_LANE_CONTAINMENT hook denies out-of-lane writes AT WRITE TIME — the flow is attribute → amend (lane widens) → write, never write-first; amendments obey the exact-file-paths-only granularity rule (no directories/globs). CARRY-FORWARD FROM #759, CORRECTED — the producer's descope note for this item was FACTUALLY WRONG and its correction is now #759 rework, not #762 work. The note claimed composed gate-role units "fail CLOSED — simply invisible to --gate-verify / --verdict-check". The adversarial pass reproduced the opposite on a clean tree: a composed code-reviewer unit is a LEGAL composition (validateComposition reserves only finalize / main-session-gate / expansion-point), it OPENS through the freeze view with a schema-1 dispatch carrying no contract_version and no review_context_hash, and it CLOSES through the execution view's schema-2 review path, so close-node refuses review_context_mismatch permanently while expand-close refuses scheduler_active and reconcile-running-set no-ops — one live node, no ready work, no legal transition. That is a HARD WEDGE, not fail-closed. The byte-identical spine reviewer in the same plan is closable, which is the two-node-view split realizing exactly the risk the producer had flagged. #759 owns the compose-time fix (reserve the gate roles in validateComposition, or widen the review-context seam) and it must ship WITH a real close-lifecycle test, because the walkthrough's close->sink tail was satisfied by literal ledger string replacement and therefore could not have caught this. WHAT REMAINS FOR #762 once #759 lands: decide whether --gate-verify / --verdict-check should see composed gate units at all, WITH tests for the G1 semantics, since widening makes G1 demand a reviewer post-dominating each composed WRITER unit — a real semantic change. Refuse it explicitly and re-assign if that is the right call; do not leave it unstated. GENERAL LESSON: a producer's own characterisation of its descope is a CLAIM, not evidence — carry the item, never the adjective. |
| #763 | feat(adaptive): shared context packet + per-expansion efficiency evidence line | queued — epic #757 WAVE B (co-opens with #761 + #760; additive adaptive-node.js hooks; merge SECOND of the three) | — | Two lightweight mechanisms, explicitly not a subsystem. (1) Orientation emits .cache/context-packet.md (goal, constraints, key files, conventions, join expectations); every frontier unit dispatch includes it verbatim — lowers per-agent setup cost, raising profitable width under the grain rule. (2) On expansion close, ONE evidence line: expansion <id>: width=<N> mode=<serial\|co_open> serializer=<none\|S1\|S2\|S3> rework=<K>; one rollup line per run in the archive summary — makes the fan-out turning point empirical. Non-goals: no token metering, no timing infra, no dashboards, no new durable files beyond the packet. CARRY-FORWARD FROM #759 (Wave A descope, owner named by the producer): workflow-tasks.json (the derived UI mirror, kaola-workflow-task-mirror.js) does not list composed units — it reads parseNodes (freeze view). Cosmetic and non-authoritative (the ## Node Ledger is the correctness truth and unit rows ARE in it), one-line fix via planNodesWithExpansions. It belongs with this child because this is the observability child; take it or explicitly decline it. |

## Rules

- A separate roadmap/research session owns discovering and adding future work to GitHub issues.
- `workflow-next` fetches GitHub issues, mirrors active implementation work here, and advances one item per cycle.
- After each workflow cycle, refresh this file from issue state.
- Move completed workflow project folders to `kaola-workflow/archive/`.
- Close linked GitHub issues only after acceptance criteria pass.
